Case title: AI multiverse / nonstandard errors in autonomous empirical analysis
Short description: Run many autonomous agents on the same data and hypothesis to estimate nonstandard errors from model and sampling variation.
Empirical unit of analysis: Independent AI-agent reports, code, estimates, and revision-stage changes on identical tasks
Candidate roles tested: Counterfactual and transportability judge; protocol and provenance architect
Key typology question: Are some human roles really about specifying forks and multiverse boundaries rather than doing the analysis itself?
Observable artifacts / traces: Agent traces, measure-choice forks, revision-stage convergence, report outputs
Key outcomes observed: Dispersion of estimates, convergence after feedback, model-family preferences
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: AI agents converge naturally to the same defensible result without multiverse management
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Tasks may overstate ambiguity if the research question is intentionally underspecified
Why this case has high leverage: It makes latent fork choices visible and quantifiable, which is rare in human-only workflows.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
